title: Tracking Email count and other metrics
excerpt:
Hello, 
Has anyone found a solution for tracking the number of emails sent, open rate, response rate, etc. I know Mass email shows these number but we are looking for the metrics from the standard emailing function withi…

posts.author: Charlie_F (Charlie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

Has anyone found a solution for tracking the number of emails sent, open rate, response rate, etc. I know Mass email shows these number but we are looking for the metrics from the standard emailing function within the leads and contacts boards.

Thanks!

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
There is a feature request for this *WARNING* Emails & Activities Automation Is Messing With Your Reports!

I’ve done some automations in the past ie a column that increases by 1 each time an email and/or reply sent and then used those numbers and a formula column to get reply rate. I’ve not found a workaround for open/click rates.

posts.author: Charlie_F (Charlie)
posts.cookedHtml: I see. Thanks Desiree. That’s the only route I can think of. My primary issue is needing to know how many emails sent during a time period. Unfortunately, that would involve adding too many date columns if I had a date set every time an email is sent

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m trying something right now on my account, I’ll let you know tomorrow if it worked!

posts.author: Charlie_F (Charlie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Welp, looks like monday just realized an activity tracker widget in beta! Thanks monday!

image1352×604 11.6 KB

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: That’s awesome! I don’t have that on my account yet so fingers crossed I get it soon.

posts.author: jessetolppa (Jesse Tolppa)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t track open rates but, I want to track the number of emails I am sending and I am doing that by copying the activity link of the email and pasting it into a custom activity called “email” every time I send an email. It’s not pretty but, I’m at least reporting accurately until they fix this issue.

posts.author: Charlie_F (Charlie)
posts.cookedHtml: Hello Jesse, go through support and ask for the widget I have a screenshot of above.

posts.author: jessetolppa (Jesse Tolppa)
posts.cookedHtml: Does it include emails in the activities? They don’t handle emails as activities, accordign to the dev team.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yep! This is what it looks like
